_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-30 07:28:30Z)**_

Looking at the diff: this is a pure UI refactor in a mockup file under `docs/vercel_mockup/`. The change:

1. Changes a label "Mobile Ready" → "Mobile First"
2. Splits an existing desktop-only table into a mobile card view + desktop table (responsive), rendering the same fields

Data flow analysis:
- All rendered values (`b.ref`, `b.passenger.name`, `b.voyageDate`, `b.vessel`, `b.agency`, `b.designation`, `b.idType`, `b.idNumber`, `b.reasonForTravel`, `b.officer`, `b.submittedAt`, `b.rejectionReason`, `b.approvedBy`) are rendered as plain JSX text — auto-escaped by React. No `dangerouslySetInnerHTML`, no URLs interpolated into `href`, no new outbound calls.
- Button handlers `approve(b.ref)` and `openReject(b.ref)` are identical to the pre-existing desktop branch — no new privileged operation, no new auth-gated action introduced.
- The "Voyage departed" lock (`isVoyageDeparted(b)`) is preserved in both new branches with the same fail-closed semantics — no sibling-path gate-parity regression.
- No registry/allowlist additions, no IaC, no logging/telemetry, no parser changes, no permission grants.

This file is the Vercel mockup (per the user's memory note) — a presentation prototype, not a runtime sink for real bookings. Even taking it at face value as a real handler, the diff introduces no new entry point, no new sink, no new tainted-data path, and no removed validator. The mobile/desktop branches render the same data via the same React text escaping and bind the same onClick handlers.

No security-relevant change.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-7 2026-05-30 07:28:34Z)**_

No findings. The change is a presentational responsive refactor: it adds a mobile card layout alongside the existing desktop table for the same `filtered` bookings list, and renames a badge label. All rendered values are React-escaped text, the approve/reject button handlers and the `isVoyageDeparted` lock are preserved unchanged in both layouts, and no new entry points, sinks, validators, allowlists, or auth paths are introduced.
